paxton-ken.text: No public data in our set connects Ken Paxton's recorded background or positions specifically to property taxes or public school funding.
No public data in our set on Ken Paxton's recorded votes or stated positions regarding property taxes or public school funding.

As of 2026-07-04, Ken Paxton's campaign had raised $7,605,209, per FEC filings. That is general campaign-finance context; it does not tell us the candidate's stance on property taxes or public school funding.

talarico-james.text: On taxes, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico explicitly calls for raising taxes on billionaires and corporations, including higher taxes on income and capital gains, ending 'buy, borrow, die' loopholes, raising corporate tax rates, and closing carried interest loopholes to fund working family tax cuts.

On education, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico opposes private school vouchers and has made opposition to school choice diversion programs central to his campaign. He supports universal pre-K, capping class sizes, and increasing public school funding.

As of 2026-07-04, James Talarico's campaign had raised $40,284,109, per FEC filings. That is general campaign-finance context; it does not tell us the candidate's stance on property taxes or public school funding.

caveats: We only show a connection to homeowner parent concerns when a candidate's own recorded background in our data set names it directly (for example, a farming or small-business background). Where no such record exists, we say so rather than guess at a candidate's likely position.

now.text: No recorded evidence in our set to project from for Ken Paxton on property taxes or public school funding.

long_term.assumption: Assumes James Talarico keeps this position and it turns into lasting policy, rather than changing later or staying just talk.
long_term.text: If James Talarico's recorded position on taxes holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping property taxes or public school funding the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.

now.text: On taxes, James Talarico's recorded position is: "Talarico explicitly calls for raising taxes on billionaires and corporations, including higher taxes on income and capital gains, ending 'buy, borrow, die' loopholes, raising corporate tax rates, and closing carried interest loopholes to fund working family tax cuts." That's what people focused on property taxes or public school funding can expect from James Talarico this term.

On education, James Talarico's recorded position is: "Talarico opposes private school vouchers and has made opposition to school choice diversion programs central to his campaign. He supports universal pre-K, capping class sizes, and increasing public school funding." That's what people focused on property taxes or public school funding can expect from James Talarico this term.
